channel.title: Data Structure on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Data Structure on Frank Huang

item.title: What is DAGs?
item.description: The word DAGs (Directed Acyclical Graphs) has been there for a while. Oozie use it to represent a serial of actions, spark use it as a serial of RDD.

item.title: What is the difference between dataframe, RDD and dataset in spark
item.description: RDD focuse more on HOW to manipulate data while Dataframe focuse more on WHAT users want to do about the data meaning dataframe will hide more internal optimization from the user. Reference https://www.quora.com/What-is-difference-between-dataframe-and-RDD

item.title: SQL B-tree index
item.description: In a nutshell B-tree, short for balanced search tree, is a generalization implementation of binary search tree. The basic idea is to How does different data manipulation impact by the b-tree data structure. insert will not be time consuming comparing to no index. deletion will have faster speed with where statement. update is basically the total time of insert and delete since update will first delete the record and then insert the modified record.

item.title: Using bitmap
item.description: Manipulating big data like 1 billion (1 billion == 1,000,000,000 == “十亿”, 1 million == 1,000,000 == “百万” which I always forget how to transform back and forth) , is quite memory consuming if we just use an array for storing it. We can either divide the file into smaller file and process them accordingly (ideas like map reduce, will talk about it later in another post) or use ‘bitmap’ and ‘hashmap’.
